title: Fix 2 standing failures caused by leaked module-level singletons (#13, #15 — triage bucket B)
intent: Batch B of the api-verify standing-failures triage. Two residual reds are pure test-ISOLATION defects: module-level singletons not reset between tests, so state leaks across the session and the test fails only in the full suite (passes in isolation). #13 the RMOS runs store; #15 the slowapi rate limiter. Both fixed in the single existing autouse fixture in services/api/tests/conftest.py. Test-only; no production code touched, no behavior change.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Both edits are in the autouse `rmos_global_test_isolation` fixture in services/api/tests/conftest.py. (#13) The store reset targeted `app.rmos.runs_v2.store._default_store`, but that name is only a STALE re-export (bound at import time) of the real singleton `app.rmos.runs_v2.store_api._default_store`, which the runs endpoints actually read via _get_default_store(). Resetting the re-export was a no-op, so RunStoreV2 pinned the first test's RMOS_RUNS_DIR and runs persisted by earlier tests leaked into test_runs_index_with_filters (counted 3 where it created 1). Changed the reset to `store_api._default_store = None`. (#15) Added rate-limiter isolation: the slowapi `limiter` is a module-level singleton with in-memory, IP-keyed storage; TestClient uses a fixed IP so per-endpoint caps (body-outline 10/hour) accumulate across the whole session and eventually 429. `enabled` is captured from env at import time, so setting RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED in a fixture is too late; instead `monkeypatch.setattr(limiter, 'enabled', False)` disables the constructed singleton per-test (monkeypatch auto-restores it, so no manual teardown).
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: Both hypotheses were re-grounded against current code. #13: confirmed store.py:396-398 re-exports the name from store_api while the endpoints (api_runs.py) read store_api's global via _get_default_store; the fixture (and the two per-test fixtures) reset the wrong module. #15: confirmed the limiter's `enabled` is baked in at construction (rate_limit.py:150-154) from an import-time env read, so only a runtime attribute toggle works; verified no test enforces a real 429 through the app (the only assert-429 calls the handler directly with a mocked exception; test_limiter_is_enabled_by_default asserts the module constant, not the .enabled attribute; a smoke test already tolerates 429). Both are the SAME class (un-reset module singleton) and share the one autouse fixture.

commands_run: services/api venv: pytest tests/test_rmos_runs_e2e.py -> 6 passed (#13; the intra-file run leak that made test_runs_index_with_filters see 3-not-1 is reset via the correct store_api singleton)
pytest tests/test_jumbo_dimension_consistency.py tests/test_body_export_bridge.py -> 39 passed, 1 skipped (#15; many body-outline POSTs in one process no longer 429 with the limiter disabled)
note: full api-verify is an Ubuntu-CI witness (fastapi 0.137 pin); local venv is fastapi 0.121.1

files.intent: #13 reset the LIVE store singleton (store_api, not the stale store re-export); #15 disable the slowapi limiter per-test via monkeypatch.setattr (auto-restored)
files.behavior_change: none - test-isolation only; no production code touched
